a-new-dark-age.script: A New Dark Age. Above you hangs a slender crescent moon against a black sky, for by the nineteenth century a new darkness had fallen upon Islam. The Prophet Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon him, had foretold such a time. He warned, as recorded in Mishkat Al-Masabih: "There will come a time upon the people when nothing will remain of Islam except its name only and nothing will remain of the Quran except its inscription. Their mosques will be splendidly furnished but destitute of guidance. Their divines will be the worst people under the heaven and strife will issue from them, and avert to them." The Holy Quran itself, in chapter twenty-five, verse thirty-one, foretells this abandonment: "And the Messenger will say, 'O my Lord, my people indeed treated this Qur'an as a discarded thing.'" Yet in the same age of decline there was also a promise of renewal, for the Prophet had asked, as recorded in Sahih Al Bukhari: "How will you be when the Son of Mary will descend upon you, and he will be the Imam from among you?" Consider now how that dark age had come upon Islam in India. In the nineteenth century, Islam was under deadly attack. For centuries, the Muslim states had given way to Christian empires. In British India, Christian missionaries were converting Muslims by the thousand, pointing to the mainstream belief that Jesus was alive in heaven, while the Prophet Muhammad had passed away. And a new Hindu group, the Arya Samaj, was undermining the Holy Prophet of Islam and the Quran. Islam was crying out for a Saviour. One man came forth. His name was Mirza Ghulam Ahmad of Qadian. He described the final struggle that this awaited Messiah would face, writing: "This Messiah who on account of the promise of God concerning his appearance is called the Promised Messiah in sacred books, would, it is foretold, be made to stand against Satan, and between him and the evil one there would be a mighty struggle, the final struggle between good and evil. For this spiritual fight Satan would bring all his hosts into the field and gather together all his power and make use of all his resources. Never shall a fight like this have occurred between good and evil before, for on that day the tricks of Satan, and the means by which he can mislead people, would all be exhausted. After a heavy fight, the Messiah of God would drive back the powers of darkness, and the glory, majesty, unity and holiness of God would be proclaimed upon earth and would continue to be declared for a thousand years, the seventh day of the Holy Books of God. Then will be the end. I am that Messiah: let him who will, accept me."

verses.text: And the Messenger will say, 'O my Lord, my people indeed treated this Qur'an as a discarded thing.'

There will come a time upon the people when nothing will remain of Islam except its name only and nothing will remain of the Quran except its inscription. Their mosques will be splendidly furnished but destitute of guidance. Their divines will be the worst people under the heaven and strife will issue from them, and avert to them.
How will you be when the Son of Mary will descend upon you, and he will be the Imam from among you?

This Messiah who on account of the promise of God concerning his appearance is called the Promised Messiah in sacred books, would, it is foretold, be made to stand against Satan, and between him and the evil one there would be a mighty struggle, the final struggle between good and evil. For this spiritual fight Satan would bring all his hosts into the field and gather together all his power and make use of all his resources. Never shall a fight like this have occurred between good and evil before, for on that day the tricks of Satan, and the means by which he can mislead people, would all be exhausted. After a heavy fight, the Messiah of God would drive back the powers of darkness, and the glory, majesty, unity and holiness of God would be proclaimed upon earth and would continue to be declared for a thousand years, the seventh day of the Holy Books of God. Then will be the end. I am that Messiah: let him who will, accept me.

the-persian-messiah.script: The Persian Messiah. Before you rises an ornate Mughal-style palace facade, a fitting backdrop for the story of a reformer of Persian origin. Consider first the ascent of faith. The Holy Quran, in chapter sixty-two, verse four, declares: "He it is Who has raised among the Unlettered people a Messenger from among themselves who recites unto them His Signs, and purifies them, and teaches them the Book and wisdom, although they were before, in manifest misguidance. And among others from among them who have not yet joined them. He is the Mighty, the Wise." A companion related: "One Day we were sitting with the Holy Prophet when Surah Jumu'ah was revealed," and the Prophet put his hand on Salman the Persian and said, as recorded in Bukhari: "If Faith were to go up to the Pleiades, a man from these would surely find it." Now consider Ahmad the Persian. The family of Hazrat Ahmad, who lived from eighteen thirty-five to nineteen hundred and eight, was of Persian origin. His ancestors settled in a region of modern Uzbekistan called Samarkand, a land that had been ruled by Persian empires and had deeply entrenched Persianate culture. He received many revelations indicating his Persian origin, among them: "A man of Persian descent has published the refutation of those who have disbelieved and have obstructed people from the path of Allah. Allah appreciates his effort. The book of the friend of Allah is the sword of 'Ali." And so we come to the champion of Allah. Born in eighteen thirty-five into a family of local chieftains, Ahmad spent his early life in solitude, studying Islamic, Christian and Hindu scriptures, and sinking deep into prayer. Though he sought to remain unknown, he eventually felt compelled to defend Islam publicly against the many attacks it was enduring. In the eighteen-eighties he released a magnificent defence of Islam entitled Barahin-e-Ahmadiyya. About this work, the cleric who would one day become his bitterest enemy wrote: "In our opinion, from the point of view of the modern age, this book stands unique in the history of Islam. No book has ever been published like it in the past, and we cannot say anything about the future, which is known only to God. The perseverance of the author in the service of Islam, through his life, his energy, pen, tongue, and every form of activity is almost unprecedented amongst Muslims. This should not be taken as Asiatic exaggeration. We challenge anyone to show us the like of this book."

verses.text: He it is Who has raised among the Unlettered people a Messenger from among themselves who recites unto them His Signs, and purifies them, and teaches them the Book and wisdom, although they were before, in manifest misguidance. And among others from among them who have not yet joined them. He is the Mighty, the Wise.
If Faith were to go up to the Pleiades, a man from these would surely find it.

A man of Persian descent has published the refutation of those who have disbelieved and have obstructed people from the path of Allah. Allah appreciates his effort. The book of the friend of Allah is the sword of 'Ali.

verses.source: The cleric who became his bitterest enemy
verses.text: In our opinion, from the point of view of the modern age, this book stands unique in the history of Islam. No book has ever been published like it in the past, and we cannot say anything about the future, which is known only to God. The perseverance of the author in the service of Islam, through his life, his energy, pen, tongue, and every form of activity is almost unprecedented amongst Muslims. prophecy-of-the-promised-messiah.script: A Prophecy Fulfilled. This board sets before you a single reflection in the words of Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad, framed within a golden arch. He writes: "Our lord and master the Holy Prophet, may peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his progeny, had declared as a prophecy that a time would come when the Muslims would acquire a strong resemblance to the Jews. Then a man of Persian origin would be raised to teach them the faith. Had faith ascended to the Pleiades, that man would have brought it down from that height. This is a prophecy of the Holy Prophet, may peace and blessings of Allah be upon him. Divine revelation has expounded the purport of this prophecy to my humble self and God the Almighty has made it clear to me by revelation that Hadrat Masih Ibn-e-Maryam, the Messiah, Son of Mary, was also a teacher of faith who appeared fourteen centuries after Hadrat Musa, that is Moses, peace be upon him. Now, it so happened that when about fourteen hundred years had passed after the advent of the Holy Prophet, on whom be the peace and blessings of Allah, the Muslims largely developed the same evil characteristics which the Jews had developed. Thus, it was necessary to fulfil the prophecy made about them. In consequence, God the Almighty, of His perfect power, has sent for them the like of the Messiah as a teacher of faith."

verses.text: Our lord and master the Holy Prophet, may peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his progeny, had declared as a prophecy that a time would come when the Muslims would acquire a strong resemblance to the Jews... Then a man of Persian origin would be raised to teach them the faith. Had faith ascended to the Pleiades, that man would have brought it down from that height. This is a prophecy of the Holy Prophet, may peace and blessings of Allah be upon him. Divine revelation has expounded the purport of this prophecy to my humble self and God the Almighty has made it clear to me by revelation that Hadrat Masih Ibn-e-Maryam (Messiah, Son of Mary) was also a teacher of faith who appeared fourteen centuries after Hadrat Musa (Moses). Now, it so happened that when about 1400 years had passed after the advent of the Holy Prophet, on whom be the peace and blessings of Allah, the Muslims largely developed the same evil characteristics which the Jews had developed. Thus, it was necessary to fulfil the prophecy made about them. In consequence, God the Almighty, of His perfect power, has sent for them the like of the Messiah as a teacher of faith.
